tools.name: acp_spawn
tools.description: Spawn an external coding agent (e.g. Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) via ACP to work on a task. The agent runs as a subprocess and streams results back. Use this when you want to delegate a coding task to an external agent that has its own tools, file editing, and terminal access. IMPORTANT: Do NOT modify the ACP config — the agent command (claude-agent-acp) is pre-configured. If ACP is not enabled or the agent is not found, report the error to the user instead of editing config.

agent.description: Which agent to spawn (e.g. 'claude', 'codex', 'gemini'). Defaults to 'claude'.

task.description: The task to give the agent — what it should accomplish

cwd.description: Working directory for the agent. This determines where the agent runs and where its session is stored. Set this to the project/repo root the user wants the agent to work in. Defaults to current session's working directory.

reason.description: Brief explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: acp_status
tools.description: Get the status of a specific ACP session or list all ACP sessions. Only use this when the user explicitly asks about ACP session status — do NOT poll automatically, as you will be notified when sessions complete.

acp_session_id.description: Optional ACP session ID to query. If omitted, returns all ACP sessions.

reason.description: Brief explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: acp_abort
tools.description: Abort a running ACP session by ID. This kills the agent process and removes the session.

acp_session_id.description: The ID of the ACP session to abort.

reason.description: Brief explanation of why this tool is being called
